free_text: Angular-correlation studies of the reaction
24Mg(alpha,pgamma)27Al
ANU tandem accelerator.
Beam of 4He++ ions.
A target consisting of
approximately 100 ug/cm2 of 24Mg (99.96% enrichment)
deposited on a thin carbon foil mounted at the centre
of a thin-walled chamber.
For all levels coincidence events were
detected by a slow-fast coincidence system using
crossover timing with a resolution of 140 ns; when
the position-sensitive detector was used, timing was
based on the energy signal.
The number of coincidence counts at each setting of
the gamma-ray detector was normalized to the total
proton counts in a window encompassing all three
levels (see REL-REF).
All gamma-ray spectra were analyzed using the line-
shape fitting procedure.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
No explanation of raw data units is given.
The data have been corrected for random coincidences.
An annular surface-barrier counter detected
protons emitted at angles between 165 and 171 degr.
to the beam axis giving an acceptance solid angle of
130 msr. Aluminium foil of total thickness 32 um
were placed in front of the counter to ensure that
the proton groups of interest were separated in energy
from scattering alpha particles.
Gamma rays were detected at angles of 20,42.5,
67.5 and 90 degr. by a 12.7 cm x 10.2 cm NaI(Tl)crystal
located 20 cm from the target spot.
No information about source of uncertainties
Fig.2(a) from Nucl.Phys.,A126(1969)49
SD: SF6=SPC -> DA/DE; SF7=G/G added;
SF8=REL/RAW -> REL in REACTION code. STATUS updated.
Accuracy of digitized data presentation revised.
No explanation of raw data units is given.

No explanation of raw data units is given.
Protons were detected using a 5 cm*6 mm
position-sensitive solid-state detector at the focal
plane of a 61 cm double-focusing magnetic
spectrometer set at 0 degr. to the beam direction.
Gamma rays were detected at angles of
90,110,135 150 and 157.5 degr. using two
12.7 cm x 10.2 cm NaI(Tl) crystals.
The data have been corrected for random coincidences
No information about source of uncertainties
